rulings.Case Title: GILBERT FORBES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 12, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Soco West, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is continued to October 13, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503 per defendant¿s notice filed August 5, which listed October 13 as the hearing date. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EMILIO VALDIVIA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 12, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Highland Stucco and Lime Products, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration, defendant¿s responses to General Order 129 interrogatories and Mr. Atkinson¿s deposition testimony create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Highland color coat stucco and Oyster White acoustical plaster) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration and its own General Order interrogatory responses are overruled. The court only considered this as a motion for summary judgment. Although defendant¿s caption titles it a motion for summary judgment or in the alternative motion for summary adjudication, defendant¿s notice and separate statement failed to state the specific causes of action or issues sought to be adjudicated in violation of CRC 3.1350(b), (d). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EMILIO VALDIVIA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 12, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant DAP, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-5 (exposure/causation) and off calendar as to issues 6-8 (false representation, civil battery and punitive damages). Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration and defendant¿s responses to General Order interrogatories create a triable issue whether plaintiff worked with and sanded DAP asbestos-containing Architectural Caulk. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production on the issue of whether plaintiff¿s sanding of the asbestos-containing DAP products released respirable fibers. Plaintiff¿s objections to Mr. Treat¿s declaration and Mr. Spencer¿s declaration are sustained. Assuming plaintiff was exposed to DAP¿s asbestos-containing products, Dr. Bruch¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his disease. Defendant¿s objections to Dr. Bruch¿s declaration are overruled. Plaintiff represented in his response separate statement that he has dismissed the false representation and civil battery causes of action and his claim for punitive damages, although the court sees no dismissal on file. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN MCHUGH et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 12, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant D. Zelinsky and Sons, Inc.¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)
